svg.title: Golden Rectangle — Scaffolded
svg.desc:
Golden rectangle with construction arc (dashed) showing how the proportions were found, plus two levels of recursion: the square cut from the outer rectangle reveals a golden rectangle, cut again reveals another.
phi = 1.61803... Short side = 400, long side = 647.2 Positioned: left=76.4, top=200, right=723.6, bottom=600
Square 1: x=76.4 to x=476.4 (width=400) Golden rect 1: x=476.4 to x=723.6 (width=247.2, height=400)
Square 2 inside golden rect 1: height=247.2, so cut at y=200+247.2=447.2 from x=476.4 to x=723.6, y=200 to y=447.2 Golden rect 2: x=476.4 to x=723.6, y=447.2 to y=600 (width=247.2, height=152.8)
Construction arc: Midpoint of left side of outer rectangle: (76.4, 400) Far corner: (476.4, 600) [bottom-right of the square] Radius = distance = sqrt((476.4-76.4)^2 + (600-400)^2) = sqrt(400^2 + 200^2) = sqrt(160000+40000) = sqrt(200000) ≈ 447.2 Arc swings from the far corner down to the baseline (y=600) Landing point x = 76.4 + 447.2 = 523.6...
Wait — the standard construction: Start with square of side s. Midpoint of bottom edge M. Radius = distance from M to top-right corner of square. Swing arc from top-right corner down to extension of bottom edge.
Let me redo with square on LEFT, oriented landscape: Square: (76.4, 200) to (476.4, 600), side=400 Midpoint of bottom edge of square: M = (276.4, 600) Top-right corner of square: C = (476.4, 200) Radius = sqrt((476.4-276.4)^2 + (200-600)^2) = sqrt(200^2+400^2) = sqrt(200000) ≈ 447.2 Arc center = M = (276.4, 600) Arc swings from C=(476.4,200) clockwise down to baseline y=600 Landing x = 276.4 + 447.2 = 723.6 ✓ (that's exactly our right edge!)
So the arc: center=(276.4,600), radius=447.2 from C=(476.4,200) sweeping clockwise to (723.6,600) Start angle from center to C: atan2(200-600, 476.4-276.4) = atan2(-400,200) ≈ -63.43° = 296.57° End angle: 0° (point to the right on baseline) SVG arc: large-arc=0, sweep=1 (clockwise)
